metadata.title: Analysis Flow Debug Double-Check — 6-Factor Factorial: Ensemble Evaluation + UQ
metadata.priority: P0 — validates ensemble evaluation pipeline for factorial experiment

context.summary:
Verify and complete the Analysis Flow (Flow 3) for the factorial experiment. Analysis Flow discovers ALL training + post-training runs from the upstream experiment (minivess_training), builds ensemble permutations, evaluates on MiniVess (trainval) and DeepVess (test), computes per-volume metrics with bootstrap CIs, and creates NEW MLflow runs in a SEPARATE experiment (minivess_evaluation) for each ensemble strategy.
The Analysis Flow contributes factor #6 (ensemble_strategy) to the 6-factor factorial design. It is defined in the factorial YAML under factors.analysis.
The Analysis Flow output is the PRIMARY INPUT to Biostatistics Flow. Each ensemble strategy (including "none" = no ensemble) is a factorial condition.
Zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) are separate from the factorial grid — they are evaluated as standalone conditions (synthesis Part 1.2).
context.synthesis-reference:
This plan is subordinate to the Pre-Debug Run Synthesis: docs/planning/intermedia-plan-synthesis-pre-debug-run.md
Key synthesis sections governing this plan: - Part 1.2 Layer C: Analysis Factors (ensemble_strategy) - Part 1.3: MVRD scope — ALL ensemble strategies run in debug (Rule #27) - Part 2.1: Flow execution order (Analysis is Flow 3) - Part 2.3: MLflow experiment names (analysis logs to minivess_evaluation, SEPARATE) - Part 2.4: MLflow tag schema (ensemble_strategy, upstream_training_run_id) - Part 3.2: Analysis Flow 10-step pipeline - Part 7.1: Complete 6-factor table (factor #6 is ensemble_strategy) - Part 8 Q2: RESOLVED — summary stats for debug, full maps for production - Part 8 Q3: RESOLVED — skip soft predictions for debug, store for production
context.full-factorial-context:
The complete 6-factor design (from pre-gcp-master-plan.xml): 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib × 3 post-training × 2 recalibration × 5 ensemble = 720
Analysis Flow contributes the FINAL factor (#6: ensemble_strategy) that completes the full factorial. It takes upstream output from Layers A+B (training + post-training) and multiplies by ensemble_strategy levels.
The factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups (configs/factorial/*.yaml). Analysis reads its factor levels from factors.analysis in the YAML:
factors: analysis: ensemble_strategy: [none, per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best] # 5
ALL factors are auto-derived from whichever factorial YAML was used. Zero hardcoded factor names in infrastructure code.

factorial-factors.expansion:
Analysis is the FINAL multiplicative layer of the full factorial:
FULL PRODUCTION: Layer A (Training): 4×3×2 = 24 cells × 3 folds = 72 training runs Layer B (Post-Training): 72 × (3 methods × 2 recalib) = 432 post-training variants Layer C (Analysis): 432 × 5 ensemble strategies = 2160 evaluation conditions + zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm)
DEBUG: Layer A: 4×3×2 = 24 × 1 fold = 24 training runs Layer B: 24 × (2 × 2) = 96 post-training variants Layer C: 96 × 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions + zero-shot baselines
Full factorial: 4×3×2×3×2×5 = 720 conditions Debug factorial: 4×3×2×2×2×4 = 384 conditions
Cost: ZERO for DynUNet (CPU inference). GPU needed only for SAM3 inference. ONLY Layer A (training, 72 runs ~$65) costs money on GPU.

ensemble-permutations.strategy: Each individual training/post-training model evaluated independently. Included in full factorial (5 levels) but dropped in debug (4 levels).

strategy.name: per_loss_single_best
strategy.description: Best fold per loss (1 checkpoint per fold x K folds)

ensemble-permutations.strategy: Per loss function, select the single best checkpoint (by primary metric) from each fold. Creates 3 ensembles (one per loss): cbdice_cldice, dice_ce, dice_ce_cldice.

strategy.name: all_loss_single_best
strategy.description: Single best model across all losses

ensemble-permutations.strategy: Select the single best checkpoint from each fold across ALL losses. Creates 1 ensemble.

strategy.name: per_loss_all_best
strategy.description: All metric checkpoints per loss per fold

ensemble-permutations.strategy: For each loss, load ALL 6 tracked metric checkpoints per fold. Maximum diversity within one loss. Creates 3 ensembles.
All losses x all folds x all metric checkpoints. Maximum ensemble diversity for epistemic uncertainty estimation. Creates 1 ensemble.
ensemble-permutations.mlflow-convention: Each ensemble strategy creates its OWN MLflow run in minivess_evaluation (RESOLVED — separate runs per strategy for cleaner Biostatistics discovery). Each run is tagged with ensemble_strategy so Biostatistics can treat it as a factorial factor (#6).

mlflow-experiment-design.analysis-experiment: minivess_evaluation (SEPARATE — analysis creates new runs here)
mlflow-experiment-design.rationale: The Analysis Flow creates MANY more runs than training (up to 2160 in production). Keeping them in the same experiment would pollute training metrics. Biostatistics discovers analysis runs from minivess_evaluation.

per-volume-averaging.correct: Average voxel-level probability maps (logits) across ensemble members -> threshold -> compute metrics
per-volume-averaging.wrong: Compute per-member metrics -> average the metrics (WRONG for segmentation)
per-volume-averaging.rationale: Metric-level averaging loses spatial information. A model that predicts vessel A correctly and misses vessel B, when averaged with a model that does the opposite, would show 50% accuracy at metric level but potentially 100% at logit level.

uq-from-deep-ensembles.total-uncertainty: H[p-bar] = entropy of mean ensemble prediction
uq-from-deep-ensembles.epistemic: MI = H[p-bar] - E[H[p]] = mutual information (model disagreement)
uq-from-deep-ensembles.storage-policy: Debug MVRD: summary statistics only (mean epistemic per volume) as MLflow metrics Production: full 5D uncertainty maps as MLflow artifacts (~50MB per volume) Reference: synthesis Part 8 Q2 (RESOLVED)

baseline.name: vesselfm
baseline.description: Foundation model, zero-shot eval. DeepVess ONLY (data leakage on MiniVess)

zero-shot-baselines.convention: Zero-shot baselines are evaluated as standalone conditions in minivess_evaluation. They are NOT part of the factorial Cartesian product (not multiplied by 720 or 384). Tagged with: model_family={name}, ensemble_strategy="none", is_zero_shot="true"

context.debug-scope:
ALL 4 ensemble strategies run in debug (the "none" strategy is dropped for debug). ALL evaluations on DeepVess. Debug means fewer epochs/data/folds and fewer factor LEVELS, NOT fewer evaluations. Inference strategy: standard_patch only (fast_patch is best-effort).
Debug total: 4×3×2×2×2×4 = 384 conditions × 1 fold = 384 evaluations. + zero-shot baselines.
context.layered-anova-context:
Analysis Flow's contribution (factor #6: ensemble_strategy) enables the FULL 6-factor ANOVA. The layered structure:
1. Core 3-way ANOVA: model_family × loss_name × aux_calibration (Layer A) 2. Extended 5-way ANOVA: + post_training_method × recalibration (Layers A+B) 3. Full ensemble analysis: + ensemble_strategy (all 6 factors, Layers A+B+C)
All factors auto-derived from the factorial YAML. Zero hardcoded factor names. All statistical parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (Rule #29).

phase.name: Data Availability — Can Analysis Find All Upstream Runs?
phase.description: Analysis must discover ALL training + post-training runs from minivess_training. In debug: 24 training + up to 96 post-training = up to 120 upstream runs. In production: 72 training + up to 432 post-training = up to 504 upstream runs.

tasks.task: Write test: Analysis discovers all training conditions from debug factorial. Debug: 24 training conditions (4 models × 3 losses × 2 calibration × 1 fold). Verify discover_training_runs_raw() finds runs tagged with model_family, loss_function. Also discovers post-training variants (tagged with post_training_method). All runs in minivess_training experiment (synthesis Part 2.3). Test both: local mlruns path AND Cloud Run MLflow API.

Write test: expand_runs_to_per_fold() creates correct per-fold entries. For K=1 debug: 24 entries. For K=3 production: 72 entries. Post-training variants expand this further (up to 96 in debug, 432 in production).
Write test: Debug mode relaxes eval_fold2_dsc requirement. When UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT starts with "debug_", require_eval_metrics=False.
Write test: zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) are discovered separately from factorial grid. They are standalone conditions.

phase.name: Ensemble Building — All Strategies from Factorial YAML
phase.description: Each ensemble strategy is factor #6 in the 6-factor table (synthesis Part 7.1). Each strategy creates its OWN MLflow run in minivess_evaluation. The ensemble strategies are READ from the factorial YAML (configs/factorial/*.yaml::factors.analysis.ensemble_strategy) — NOT hardcoded.

tasks.task: Write test: per_loss_single_best creates 3 ensembles (one per loss).
Write test: all_loss_single_best creates 1 ensemble with L x K members.
Write test: per_loss_all_best creates 3 ensembles with K x 6 members each.
Write test: all_loss_all_best creates 1 full deep ensemble.

Write test: Each ensemble strategy creates a SEPARATE MLflow run in minivess_evaluation, tagged with ensemble_strategy={name}. This is the mechanism by which Biostatistics treats ensemble_strategy as factorial factor #6.

Write test: post-training variants (SWA, calibrated) are included as candidate members for ensemble building. EnsembleBuilder queries minivess_training (which contains both training and post-training runs).
Write test: ensemble strategies are read from the factorial YAML (factors.analysis.ensemble_strategy), not hardcoded in the flow code. A different lab could define a custom set of strategies.

phase.name: Per-Volume Evaluation — Correct Averaging
phase.description: Per-volume metrics are ESSENTIAL for Biostatistics Flow. They enable Wilcoxon signed-rank tests and specification curve analysis. Averaging must happen at LOGIT level, not metric level (synthesis Part 1.2 Layer C).

tasks.task: Write test: Ensemble predictions average LOGITS, not metrics. Verify _EnsembleInferenceWrapper.
Write test: per-volume metrics logged to MLflow (eval/{fold}/vol/{vol_id}/{metric}).
Write test: External test evaluation on DeepVess (test/deepvess/all/{metric}).
Write test: Bootstrap CIs computed for all metrics. CI parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (not hardcoded).

phase.name: Uncertainty Quantification from Deep Ensembles
phase.description: UQ from deep ensembles (Lakshminarayanan et al., 2017). Debug: summary statistics only. Production: full maps. Reference: synthesis Part 8 Q2 (RESOLVED).

tasks.task: Write test: predict_with_uncertainty() returns total, aleatoric, epistemic maps.
Write test: epistemic = total - aleatoric (clamped >= 0).
Write test: uncertainty summaries logged as MLflow metrics. Debug: summary stats only (mean, std, max per volume). Production: full 5D maps stored as artifacts.

phase.name: MLflow Run Creation — Separate Experiment
phase.description: Analysis creates runs in minivess_evaluation (SEPARATE from training). Reference: synthesis Part 2.3. Each ensemble strategy = one run. Debug: up to 384 evaluation runs. Production: up to 2160 evaluation runs.

tasks.task: Write test: Analysis creates runs in minivess_evaluation (not minivess_training). Verify experiment name matches synthesis Part 2.3.

Write test: Each run tagged with flow_name="analysis-flow", ensemble_strategy={name}, upstream metadata (model_family, loss_function, etc.). Tag schema matches synthesis Part 2.4. All 6 experimental factors discoverable from tags for layered ANOVA.

Write test: Run naming enables Biostatistics discovery. Biostatistics queries minivess_evaluation to find all analysis runs. The layered ANOVA (3-way → 5-way → 6-factor) can extract all factor columns from the tags.
Write test: zero-shot baseline evaluation runs are tagged with is_zero_shot="true" and ensemble_strategy="none".
Write test: Champion uses compound_masd_cldice as primary metric. Champion selection metric from config, not hardcoded (Rule #29).
Write test: Champion tags written to mlruns/ filesystem.

phase.name: Soft Predictions Storage Policy
phase.description: Per-volume soft predictions (probability maps) storage policy. Reference: synthesis Part 8 Q3 (RESOLVED).

tasks.task: Write test: Debug MVRD does NOT store per-volume probability maps. Only binary masks + threshold metadata are stored.
Write test: Production mode stores per-volume probability maps as MLflow artifacts for post-hoc threshold sweeps.
Run Analysis Flow locally on downloaded DynUNet artifacts (CPU-only). Expected: 96 post-training variants × 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation runs. Cost: ZERO (CPU inference for DynUNet).

Verify Biostatistics Flow discovers Analysis Flow outputs from minivess_evaluation. All 6 factorial factors (model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration, post_training_method, recalibration, ensemble_strategy) discoverable via tags. Layered ANOVA can extract all factor columns.

appendix.title: Complete 6-Factor Table (synthesis Part 7.1)
appendix.content:
Analysis Flow contributes factor #6 — the FINAL experimental factor:
| # | Factor | Layer | Full Levels | Debug Levels | Compute | Config Section | |---|-------------------------|--------------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|------------------------| | 1 | model_family | A (Training) | 4 | 4 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 2 | loss_name | A (Training) | 3 | 3 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 3 | aux_calibration | A (Training) | 2 | 2 | GPU (GCP) | factors.training | | 4 | post_training_method | B (Post-Training) | 3 | 2 | CPU (local) | factors.post_training | | 5 | recalibration | B (Post-Training) | 2 | 2 | CPU (local) | factors.post_training | | 6 | ensemble_strategy | C (Analysis) | 5 | 4 | CPU (local) | factors.analysis |
Full product: 4 × 3 × 2 × 3 × 2 × 5 = 720 conditions × 3 folds = 2160 evaluations Debug product: 4 × 3 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 4 = 384 conditions × 1 fold = 384 evaluations
Cost: ONLY Layer A runs on GPU (~$65 for 72 runs). Layers B+C run on local CPU (free). Analysis Flow (Layer C) is the final multiplier that completes the full factorial.
Analytical factors (#7-#13) are researcher degrees of freedom for specification curve (Layer D: Biostatistics). See synthesis Part 7.1 for the full 13-factor table.

appendix.title: Factorial Expansion Math — How 24 Training Runs Become 384 Debug Evaluations
appendix.content:
Layer A (Training, GPU): 4 models × 3 losses × 2 aux_calib = 24 cells × 1 fold (debug) = 24 training runs
Layer B (Post-Training, CPU): 24 runs × {none, swa} × {none, temperature_scaling} = 24 × 2 × 2 = 96 variants
Layer C (Analysis, CPU): 96 variants × 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions
Full debug factorial: 4 × 3 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 4 = 384 + zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) evaluated separately
For PRODUCTION: Layer A: 24 × 3 folds = 72 runs Layer B: 72 × 3 × 2 = 432 variants Layer C: 432 × 5 = 2160 evaluations Full factorial: 4 × 3 × 2 × 3 × 2 × 5 = 720 × 3 folds = 2160

synthesis.relation: authoritative synthesis — this plan is subordinate

plan.relation: downstream consumer (layered ANOVA on all 6 factors)
